paragraph 1  
a condition that allows pregnancy to occur without the baby being delivered or showing signs of growth is known as a hidden pregnancy. this condition is also referred to as a concealed or silent pregnancy. in some cases, the placenta may not form properly, leading to a lack of fetal development. the absence of visible signs of pregnancy can make it difficult to detect the condition through routine checks. the exact cause of this condition is not fully understood, but it is believed to be related to genetic or hormonal factors.  

paragraph 2  
the condition is often diagnosed through advanced imaging techniques such as ultrasound or magnetic resonance imaging. in some cases, the baby may be detected through a blood test that measures specific markers. the presence of this condition can have implications for the health of both the mother and the baby, requiring close monitoring and specialized care. the condition is not common, but it is a real possibility in certain medical circumstances. data
